_comment: Verified live 2026-07-11 (glassdoor.ca, jobs split-pane view). CRITICAL scoping rule: on the jobs page there is a left list of many job cards AND a right detail pane showing the currently-OPEN job. A global querySelector for a company/id class matches the FIRST left-list card, NOT the open job — this caused applications to be attributed to whichever job auto-opened on page load. The fix is that all selectors below target elements that exist ONLY in the detail pane: 'JobDetails_employerAndJobTitle' (the header block wrapping the open job's employer h4 + title h1) and the h1's own id 'jd-job-title-{listingId}'. Glassdoor uses CSS Modules (stable readable prefix, churning hash suffix), so '[class*="prefix"]' partial matches are the right tool. Do NOT reintroduce a bare '[class*="EmployerName"]' selector — that matches the left-list compact cards and reopens the wrong-job bug.
confirmationTextMatches: your application has been successfully submitted
